license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wyqffvw
title: Detection of anti-phytopathogenic fungal activity
author: Cecilia Monica Rodriguez García

steps_raw.instruction: Performs three dilutions from the 10 % aqueous extract (AE) %s %s CS- conidial suspension (200 conidium/μL) Do not add de CS, only make the mix of AE and water, CS is added tube by tube

Add to a sterile 1.5 mL eppendorf tube mix 1 and then 12.5 μL of CS (in duplicate) Repeat num 1 for the next concentratión and so on.. For the 10% AE just put in each tube 125 μL 10% AE plus 12.5 μL of CS, (in duplicate) The positive control is prepared by adding 1 μL of a commercial fungicide to 125 μL of water and 12.5 μL of CS (in duplicate) Two negative controls: one is only 125 μL of water and the other is 125 μL of PDB and both with 12.5 μL of CS (in duplicate)
Once the conidial suspension has been added, homogenize very well, seal the tubes and leave them in the dark at a temperature of 26-28 ° C for 3 days.
After three days, 25 μL of the suspension is transferred in the center of the PDA medium (polystyrene dish, Corning), in duplicate. The suspension of each eppendorf tube must be perfectly homogenized

Petri dishes are incubated at 26 ± 2 °C at 12/12 photoperiod for 7 days (two replicates /concentration) Evaluation are made on day 7 If the aqueous extract of leaves is fungicidal there should be no growth on the PDA medium, if the extract is not fungicidal then there must be growth of the fungus on the PDA medium
